title: Rooms By The Hour

jokes.text: Is it a job? / I don't know, do I look like Ginnifer?!
jokes.setup: David asks if it's a job, expecting a simple yes/no answer
jokes.punchline: Alexis sarcastically asks if she looks like his agent
jokes.explanation: Strong character-specific irritation from Alexis, efficiently delivered sarcasm that reveals their dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Do I look like your agent?

jokes.text: David, your inability to retain information that doesn't directly pertain to you might actually require medical attention!
jokes.setup: David shows his typical self-centeredness about his agent
jokes.punchline: Alexis diagnoses his selfishness as a medical condition
jokes.explanation: Excellent character observation delivered as medical diagnosis - perfectly captures David's narcissism while being genuinely witty

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David, I think you have a very serious medical condition.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What? What are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It's called narcissistic personality disorder, and it's terminal.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's not funny.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: No, it's not. It's actually very sad. For everyone around you.

jokes.text: David there's nothing here but... 'hot singles in my area.'
jokes.setup: Alexis checking David's email for important agent correspondence
jokes.punchline: Only finds spam dating ads
jokes.explanation: Standard spam email joke, mildly funny but not particularly original or character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David, I'm checking your email for important agent correspondence.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God. It's just spam dating ads.

jokes.text: Attractive and spunky forty-something female. / In my sleep!
jokes.setup: Reading casting call description
jokes.punchline: Moira's immediate confident response
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Moira's theatrical confidence delivered with maximum economy

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It says 'must be willing to cry on command.' Can you cry on command?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Alexis, I've been crying on command since my second husband left me for his pilates instructor.

jokes.text: The Crows Have Eyes... Two. / It's a sequel, that's good. It must mean the first one was a big success. / I've never heard of it.
jokes.setup: Moira excited about sequel, suggesting success
jokes.punchline: David's deadpan admission he's never heard of the original
jokes.explanation: Nice deflation of Moira's excitement through David's characteristic honesty

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The sequel is coming out next week. I'm very excited about it.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That's so cool, mom!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, well, the first one was quite successful, so naturally they're capitalizing on that momentum.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I've never actually seen the first one.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I've never heard of it.

jokes.text: And so it begins again.
jokes.setup: Moira getting excited about acting opportunity
jokes.punchline: David's weary resignation to another Moira drama
jokes.explanation: Perfect long-suffering reaction from David who knows what Moira's acting enthusiasm brings

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mother, please. We've been through this. Every time you get offered a role, it's somehow going to require us to completely upend our lives.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: But David, this could be the opportunity of a lifetime! A commercial for artisanal cheese!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's a commercial. For cheese. And somehow I know this means I'll be driving you to auditions at 5 AM, you'll need a new headshot, and Patrick will have to reschedule his entire week.

jokes.text: My associate usually works the computer, But uh... let's get you booked.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to handle booking alone
jokes.punchline: His transparent excuse-making while clearly struggling
jokes.explanation: Johnny's incompetence masked by false confidence creates cringe humor

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm doing fine. I've got everything under control. I know exactly what I'm doing.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, well, the system here is very... it's complicated. Very complicated. Not like other agencies.

jokes.text: Are you new at this? / Ish. (Laughs)
jokes.setup: Guest notices Johnny's obvious struggles
jokes.punchline: Johnny's 'ish' admission with nervous laugh
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement delivered with Johnny's characteristic nervous energy

dialogue.speaker: Guest
dialogue.line: You seem like you're... struggling a bit.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I wouldn't say struggling, I'd say I'm... dealing with some challenges. Ish.

jokes.text: Who's the other actress? / Well, they haven't found her yet.
jokes.setup: Question about competition for the role
jokes.punchline: Reveals there is no actual competition yet
jokes.explanation: Nice deflation of the perceived competition, reveals the low-budget nature of the production

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Wait, so there's no competition yet?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Not yet, dear. But I'm sure once word gets out that I'm auditioning, every actress in this town will be scrambling for the role.

jokes.text: I'm sure you've seen 'The Crows Have Eyes.' / I have never heard of it, but congratulations.
jokes.setup: Moira assumes everyone knows this movie
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's blunt honesty followed by polite congratulations
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to David's earlier line, deflates Moira's assumption about the movie's fame

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, everyone knows this movie.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I don't.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: But congratulations.

jokes.text: God, it'd be such big shoes to fill. / No, Twyla, no. No deprecatory mocking.
jokes.setup: Twyla expressing humility about taking Moira's part
jokes.punchline: Moira's theatrical prohibition of self-deprecation with fancy vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - overwrought vocabulary for simple concept, character-specific coaching style

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: I'm just so honored to be taking your part. I mean, I know I'm not nearly as talented or sophisticated as you.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Twyla, stop. I will not have you disparaging yourself in my presence. It's unseemly and frankly, it's giving very little to the room.

jokes.text: He looks like if Ricky Martin and Mark Consuelos had a baby.
jokes.setup: Looking at competitor vet's photo
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific celebrity hybrid description
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and unexpected celebrity comparison that perfectly captures Alexis's pop culture references

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, he looks like if Ryan Gosling and a German Shepherd had a baby.

jokes.text: Why should the person who is performing surgery on your pet have to have a six pack?! / You have a six pack.
jokes.setup: Ted's righteous indignation about sexy vet marketing
jokes.punchline: Alexis points out his hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Ted's moral high ground with simple factual observation

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I mean, it's inappropriate. Using sex to sell veterinary services? It's exploitative and frankly, it cheapens the profession.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Ted, you literally have a poster of that sexy vet in your office.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: That's different. That's for... educational purposes.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It's literally covering your filing cabinet.

jokes.text: That's not the point! And... it's not a full six yet, That's why I'm shredding right now.
jokes.setup: Being called out for having abs
jokes.punchline: Defensive admission he's actively working on his abs
jokes.explanation: Ted's vanity undermines his moral stance perfectly, using fitness terminology seriously

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not just sitting around doing nothing. I'm actively working on my abs.

jokes.text: If he wants to sell sex, then he should just get a job down at the Dude Cave! He'd make more money than being a vet!
jokes.setup: Ted's continued outrage about sexy marketing
jokes.punchline: Suggests competitor should become male stripper
jokes.explanation: Ted's escalation to strip club suggestion is funny but feels slightly out of character

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I mean, if you're going to use sex to sell your business, why don't you just become a male stripper?

jokes.text: Um, what's the Dude Cave? / It's the all male strip club outside of town. My cousin Dwayne works there; he drives a Corvette.
jokes.setup: Innocent question about the Dude Cave
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact explanation with specific family connection and car detail
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly casual delivery of absurd small-town detail, with specific Corvette detail that sells the success angle

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What's the Dude Cave?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: It's where your dad hangs out. He's been working on his car down there.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, which car?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: The Cadillac. He says it's a classic.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That car is older than me. Like, significantly.

jokes.text: That is very popular with people who hate their jobs, or are in loveless marriages.
jokes.setup: Discussing bunny cam popularity
jokes.punchline: Alexis's casual assessment of the target demographic
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny and oddly specific demographic analysis delivered with Alexis's characteristic casualness

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, it's mostly just lonely men watching a bunny eat lettuce for eight hours a day.

jokes.text: Start from five, you leave out the two, and the one. / Okay, why? / Because it's my process.
jokes.setup: David asking how to count Moira in for her audition
jokes.punchline: Moira's arbitrary and illogical counting 'process'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - complete theatrical nonsense delivered with utter conviction, brilliantly character-specific

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: How do you want me to count you in?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, just say 'one, two, three, go.'

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Okay. One, two, three, go.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: No, that's not right. Try again.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: One, two, three, go.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You're saying the numbers too evenly. I need you to say 'one' like it's a question, 'two' like it's a statement, and then skip 'three' and go straight to 'four.'

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's not how counting works.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, it's how *I* count in.

jokes.text: Oh, you'll see! And it's not a flock, my dear boy... it's a murder.
jokes.setup: Acting out dramatic crow movie scene
jokes.punchline: Moira's theatrical delivery of the collective noun for crows
jokes.explanation: Moira's over-the-top delivery makes a simple fact sound ominous and theatrical

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A murder of crows.

jokes.text: Sorry, um, this is... just one big rip off of 'The Birds!' / This is about crows! It's much more specific!
jokes.setup: David pointing out obvious plagiarism
jokes.punchline: Moira's defensive insistence that crows make it original
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Moira's willful blindness to obvious flaws when she wants something

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You realize this is just 'The Raven,' right?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: No, it's not. There are crows in it.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: There's one crow. In 'The Raven.'

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I have several.

jokes.text: 'Look, they've taken Clara!' 'Where are they carrying her to, Clara?' Are there two Claras?!
jokes.setup: Reading ridiculous dialogue from script
jokes.punchline: David's logical confusion about the name repetition
jokes.explanation: Good critique of bad writing, David's literal-minded analysis of obviously poor dialogue

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Why would they name two characters the same thing? That's confusing.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It's not confusing, darling. One is pronounced differently.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: They're spelled the same way.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, yes, but the *intent* is different.

jokes.text: It's a common name. / Yeah, the next scene takes place in a large bird's nest.
jokes.setup: Moira defending the script's logic
jokes.punchline: David revealing even more absurd plot point
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation showing how absurd the script gets, David's deadpan delivery sells it

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The logic of the script is actually quite sound.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, the main character's love interest is a sentient bottle of pinot grigio.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, and it works.

jokes.text: I actually think you do play off a corpse in the next scene, and... if I'm being perfectly honest, this is all playing a bit big.
jokes.setup: David giving Moira acting feedback
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning she literally acts with a corpse, then understated critique
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of absurd script detail with understated acting critique, perfect David delivery

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, you literally act with a corpse, so...

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not sure what you're looking for from me here.

jokes.text: A note, coming from my son?! Who I carried for almost a full seven months
jokes.setup: Moira outraged at David giving her acting notes
jokes.punchline: Reveals David was born significantly premature as part of her credentials
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly unexpected reveal used as theatrical ammunition, perfect Moira oversharing with dark implication

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, I have been acting since before you were born. In fact, I have been acting since significantly before you were born.

jokes.text: Who I carried for almost a full seven months
jokes.setup: Moira's indignation at David giving her notes
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that David was born prematurely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark detail casually dropped in the middle of an argument. Shows Moira's narcissism while revealing family history in the most inappropriate context.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, I don't appreciate you giving me notes on my performance. I am a trained professional.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, you were born three weeks early.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, that explains it.

jokes.text: Your new partner just booked three rooms, before you even got in this morning. / Wow. Ha!
jokes.setup: Johnny proudly announcing his business success
jokes.punchline: Stevie's flat, sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Classic Stevie deadpan response to Johnny's misguided enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I've finally turned this business around. We're profitable, we're thriving, people actually respect what we've built here.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah. Cool.

jokes.text: So you're renting our rooms by the hour?! / We haven't had a guest in four days!
jokes.setup: Stevie realizes what Johnny has done
jokes.punchline: Johnny's desperate justification
jokes.explanation: Johnny's business desperation leads him to inadvertently enable prostitution

jokes.text: But when it turns out I'm right, and it's just a group of business professionals decompressing for a few hours, during a... long day at a regional accountants' conference, then I expect a full apology.
jokes.setup: Johnny defending his interpretation of the situation
jokes.punchline: His increasingly specific and naive description that obviously describes sex work
jokes.explanation: Johnny's willful naivety creates perfect cringe comedy as he describes obvious prostitution in business terms

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm simply describing what I observed. A woman, providing companionship services, meeting gentlemen in hotel rooms for substantial fees, with a clear understanding that certain... physical favors would be exchanged for compensation.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's a legitimate business arrangement. She sets her rates, they negotiate the terms, and then they spend time together in a private setting where—

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What? I'm just saying she's an entrepreneur. A very flexible entrepreneur who specializes in making men happy for money.

jokes.text: You don't hear it, when you say it like that? / I hear it a bit.
jokes.setup: Stevie pointing out how Johnny's description sounds
jokes.punchline: Johnny's minimal admission of awareness
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper to the scene with Johnny's tiny admission and Stevie's characteristic understatement

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: That sounds like you're describing yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I'm aware.

jokes.text: This isn't one of those animal rights viral videos where the bunnies go through a meat processor in the end, is it?
jokes.setup: David seeing cute bunny cam
jokes.punchline: His immediate jump to darkest possible outcome
jokes.explanation: Perfect David paranoia turning innocent situation dark, character-specific anxiety manifesting as specific fear

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is that a bunny? That's adorable.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait, why is someone filming a bunny 24 hours a day? That's not normal. Something's wrong. That bunny is definitely dead.

jokes.text: This isn't one of those animal rights viral videos where the bunnies go through a meat processor in the end, is it?
jokes.setup: David looking at cute bunny cam
jokes.punchline: David's unexpectedly dark concern about the video's ending
jokes.explanation: Dark turn that's very David - taking something innocent and imagining the worst possible outcome. Specific enough to be genuinely disturbing.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: This is adorable. Wait, where is this going? Is this... is this going to end with the bunny being eaten by a hawk?

jokes.text: That's like, double your Instagram followers. / Okay, my account is private, thanks.
jokes.setup: Alexis comparing bunny cam viewers to David's followers
jokes.punchline: David's defensive clarification about his privacy settings
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic with David's defensive response about social media popularity

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: The bunny cam has like, way more viewers than your Instagram.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, my Instagram is private, so...

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It's not private, David. I follow you, and I'm not even following you.

jokes.text: No, Ted is getting naked on your webcam!
jokes.setup: Alexis doesn't believe Ted is undressing
jokes.punchline: David's panicked clarification
jokes.explanation: Escalates the bunny cam situation effectively but fairly straightforward comedy

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: He's not undressing, Alexis. He's just... removing his clothes.

jokes.text: Ted looks good. / Yeah, he does. / David, he looks really good. / Okay, that's gross.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Ted's accidental strip show
jokes.punchline: David's disgusted reaction to sister appreciating boyfriend's body
jokes.explanation: Standard sibling discomfort with appropriate character reactions

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, did you see his body? It was amazing.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No, and I'm going to spend the rest of my life trying to unsee what I did see.

jokes.text: And that's something we didn't discuss. / And?
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to address the prostitution delicately
jokes.punchline: Sex worker's completely unbothered response
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Johnny's discomfort and the professional's matter-of-fact attitude

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I understand you work in... a particular field of business.

dialogue.speaker: Trish
dialogue.line: Oh honey, I don't work in a field. I work in a lot of positions.

jokes.text: We're a very high-end operation, and very discreet.
jokes.setup: Johnny worried about the nature of the business
jokes.punchline: Sex worker's professional pride and assurance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing professional approach to sex work

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What exactly is it that you do?

dialogue.speaker: Trish
dialogue.line: I'm a sex worker, John. And I'm very good at what I do.

dialogue.speaker: Trish
dialogue.line: I have a very high client retention rate.

jokes.text: Besides, people are making love in these rooms, whether they rent by the day, the week, or the hour.
jokes.setup: Johnny's moral concerns about the rooms' use
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact point about hotel rooms generally
jokes.explanation: Smart point about the reality of hotel business delivered with perfect logic

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm concerned about what goes on in these rooms.

dialogue.speaker: Trish
dialogue.line: Well, people sleep in them, they shower, sometimes they have sex...

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, well—

dialogue.speaker: Trish
dialogue.line: It's a hotel room, Johnny. That's what they're for.

jokes.text: people are making love in these rooms, whether they rent by the day, the week, or the hour.
jokes.setup: Being confronted about the prostitution operation
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact response that deflates the moral high ground
jokes.explanation: Excellent point that completely undercuts the moral objection. Shows the practical reality that makes Johnny and Stevie uncomfortable.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Are you running a prostitution ring out of our home?

dialogue.speaker: Trish
dialogue.line: Oh honey, it's not a ring. It's more of a... loose network.

jokes.text: Oh no, why didn't you tell me the camera could see me when I get back from my runs?! / I just noticed!
jokes.setup: Ted realizing he's been accidentally stripping on camera
jokes.punchline: Alexis claiming she just noticed (implying she watched)
jokes.explanation: Continues the webcam situation with appropriate embarrassment and implied voyeurism

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Wait, you're telling me I've been on camera this whole time?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I literally just noticed.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Just noticed?! Alexis, I've been undressing for the past ten minutes!

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know, I was... multitasking.

jokes.text: Although there was a moment last night, where it looked like you might have known, and you were doing this like, thing with your hips.
jokes.setup: Discussing Ted's accidental exposure
jokes.punchline: Alexis suggesting Ted was deliberately being sexy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ted's embarrassment with specific detail about his movements

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, Ted was literally just standing there with his shirt off. Like, he wasn't even trying to hide it.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Perhaps he was making a statement.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: A statement? Mom, the only statement he was making was 'I have a body and I'm not afraid to show it.' Which, honestly, is kind of sexy.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's really not.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I thought it was intentional. Like, who takes their shirt off that casually? He was obviously trying to seduce someone.

jokes.text: People were very concerned as to whether or not you would get your pants on successfully.
jokes.setup: Discussing website traffic spike during Ted's undressing
jokes.punchline: Alexis's clinical description of viewer interest
jokes.explanation: Perfectly understated description of voyeuristic behavior disguised as innocent concern

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, people are clearly interested in seeing Ted without his clothes on. I think that's pretty normal.

jokes.text: Well... who's to say that people weren't watching for the bunnies? / I am, that's what I'm saying.
jokes.setup: Ted trying to maintain dignity about webcam viewers
jokes.punchline: Alexis's blunt contradiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Ted's hopeful self-deception with Alexis's characteristic directness

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, I like to think of them as my viewers. People who tune in regularly to watch me work.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: They're perverts, Ted.

jokes.text: Well, what can I say, sometimes I like to live dangerously. While still respecting my personal boundaries.
jokes.setup: Ted deciding to keep the webcam running despite exposure risk
jokes.punchline: His immediate qualification about boundaries
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ted character moment - trying to be edgy but immediately hedging with his cautious nature

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I'm going to keep the webcam running. I mean, I have nothing to hide. Well, I have some things to hide, but nothing that would be of interest to anyone watching.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Although, I do need to establish some boundaries with my mother about when she can call.

jokes.text: You booked it. / Ohhhh! David! I did?
jokes.setup: David reading Moira's email about the movie role
jokes.punchline: Moira's over-the-top celebration before knowing details
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moira's theatrical excitement before getting any details

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, you haven't even read the script yet.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, I don't need to read a script. I need to read a contract. And a call sheet. And some very flattering behind-the-scenes photography.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: But you don't know anything about the role.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I know it's going to be transformative. I can feel it in my soul. This is my moment, David. This is everything I've been working toward.

jokes.text: Ooh, it's filming in Bosnia! Um, in a city that I don't, I don't know the name of the city, I can't pronounce it, a lot of consonants.
jokes.setup: Reading movie contract details to Moira
jokes.punchline: David's struggle with pronunciation and description of consonants
jokes.explanation: Good detail about the low-budget nature of the production with David's characteristic commentary

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It says here that you'll be required to pronounce words with the letter 'X' in them.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, that's not a problem. I'm quite good with my consonants.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: 'X' is not a consonant, Mother. It's a cry for help.

jokes.text: You'll be put up with a local family.
jokes.setup: Reading accommodation details from contract
jokes.punchline: Revealing the unprofessional accommodation arrangement
jokes.explanation: Continues revealing how terrible this movie deal is with specific awful detail

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It says here that my accommodations include a cot in the storage room and a mini-fridge that hasn't worked since 2003.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, David, that's what you get for working in the family business.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: A cot in a storage room isn't an accommodation, Moira. It's a cry for help.

jokes.text: There's a death waiver?! / Ohh!
jokes.setup: David continuing to read contract details
jokes.punchline: Discovery of actual death waiver requirement
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of how dangerous and unprofessional this production is, death waiver is brilliantly absurd

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait, it says here that I have to sign a death waiver.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, yes. It's a standard clause.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: A death waiver? For what, exactly?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: In case you die.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: In case I die? During what? A fitting?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, one never knows, David.

jokes.text: Yeah, but we haven't even gotten to the section on bird safety yet! / Shut it! Now.
jokes.setup: David wanting to continue reading terrible contract details
jokes.punchline: Moira's sharp command to stop after hearing enough
jokes.explanation: Great culmination of the contract reading with Moira's decisive end to the torture

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, there's also a clause here about non-compete agreements, and it says that I'm not allowed to—

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Stop.

jokes.text: when I first heard that Stevie was hitching her wagon to your cart, the first thing that came into my mind was, whoooa, Nelly! That horse is in big trouble.
jokes.setup: Roland trying to give Johnny a compliment
jokes.punchline: His convoluted metaphor that makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland moment - attempting wisdom but creating nonsensical metaphor mixing, very character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know, John, you're like a... you're like a baked potato that's been left out in the sun too long, and then someone puts it back in the oven, but they forget to take the foil off, so it's all steamed and weird, but... but in a good way. You know? You're doing great, buddy.

jokes.text: A convoluted metaphor, but um...
jokes.setup: Roland's confusing horse/cart/wagon analogy
jokes.punchline: Johnny's polite but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to Roland's absurdity, Johnny's diplomatic criticism

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I have no idea what you just said, and I'm not going to pretend that I do.

jokes.text: we don't talk about our feelings, we keep 'em all rawrrr inside!
jokes.setup: Roland imitating Johnny's supposed emotional repression
jokes.punchline: His dramatic gesture and sound effect
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Roland's theatrical interpretation and physical comedy

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I'm Johnny Rose, I don't have feelings. *makes exaggerated closing gesture* Whoooosh!

jokes.text: Who's Ken? / Well, Ken is probably her husband. / Uh huh, and she doesn't know what he looks like?
jokes.setup: Sex worker asking about client Ken
jokes.punchline: Roland pointing out the obvious issue with Johnny's interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good logic pointing out Johnny's willful blindness about what's obviously happening

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Ken's not a client, he's a friend. He comes by to visit.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, he's paying you, isn't he?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: He's paying for the room! That doesn't make him a... that makes him a guest at my motel, you absolute cabbage.

jokes.text: Well, I'm really happy to hear that, because I would hate to have to assert my mayoral authority in a situation like this, yes?
jokes.setup: Johnny promising to shut down the operation
jokes.punchline: Roland's pompous threat about his mayoral authority
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Roland's self-importance about his limited authority

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You listen here. I'm the mayor of this town, and I have half a mind to shut this whole operation down myself.

jokes.text: Well, I'm really happy to hear that, because I would hate to have to assert my mayoral authority in a situation like this, yes?
jokes.setup: Roland discovering the prostitution situation
jokes.punchline: Roland's pompous threat using made-up authority
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland pomposity. 'Assert my mayoral authority' over prostitution is exactly his kind of overreach.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I'm gonna call the mayor and tell him what's going on here. And if he doesn't do anything about it, I'm gonna call the mayor of the next town over.

jokes.text: Well, you know what, I'm glad I helped you fix your uh, broken moral compass. / Well, there's nothing wrong with my moral compass, but... / All right, well, why don't we tell Kitty that?
jokes.setup: Roland being self-righteous about Johnny's situation
jokes.punchline: Roland's suggestion to confront the sex worker about morality
jokes.explanation: Roland's moral superiority complex creates appropriate cringe with his suggestion

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know, Johnny, if you're going to consort with that type of woman, the least you could do is have a conversation with her about her life choices and morality.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, I'm not going to lecture a sex worker about her career.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, that's the problem with your generation. No moral compass.

jokes.text: while the Bosnian Riviera is a majestic locale, it can't hold a candle to singing... with you wonderful working women here, in our makeshift rehearsal studio.
jokes.setup: Moira explaining why she's returning to the duet
jokes.punchline: Her overwrought rationalization for settling for local performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira spin on her career disappointment, turning rejection into noble choice

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm returning to the duet because I've come to realize that not every artist needs a massive platform to make an impact. Sometimes the most meaningful performances happen in intimate venues where you can really connect with your audience on a deeper level. Plus, I've always believed that true artistry transcends geography. A Rose by any other name would smell as sweet, and a Moira by any other spotlight would still shine just as brightly. Besides, David mentioned something about tax write-offs, and I think we should all be supporting local business.

jokes.text: John and I made David to that song, in Pusitano.
jokes.setup: Moira explaining her connection to 'Islands in the Stream'
jokes.punchline: TMI about David's conception with incorrect geography
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly inappropriate overshare delivered with Moira's characteristic mix of romance and geographic confusion

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Your father and I were in the Caribbean, on a boat, and we were listening to 'Islands in the Stream,' and well... let's just say that's how David came to be.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, I don't need to hear this.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: We were docked in Bermuda at the time, so technically you were conceived in Bermuda.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Please stop talking.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Though your father insists it was the Bahamas.

jokes.text: ♪ K-H-E-O-K! ♪
jokes.setup: Attempting to perform 'Islands in the Stream'
jokes.punchline: Random letter singing that makes no musical sense
jokes.explanation: Good absurd musical moment showing the amateur nature of their performance

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Islands in the stream, that is what we are...

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: No one in between, how can we be wrong?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Bzzzzt, flamingo, pterodactyl...

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Sparkle, hamster, encyclopedia!

jokes.text: (Moira sings in another language) / (Moira sings loudly)
jokes.setup: Attempting to join the duet performance
jokes.punchline: Moira's theatrical oversinging in foreign language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira finale - overwhelming a simple duet with theatrical foreign language oversinging

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'd like to join the duet performance.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Amore, amore, amore! *singing dramatically in Italian* Non, non, non! *switching to French with exaggerated vibrato* Oui, oui, c'est magnifique!

jokes.text: (Moira sings loudly in another language)
jokes.setup: Jocelyn and Twyla performing their simple duet
jokes.punchline: Moira unable to resist joining in with over-the-top foreign language vocals
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending moment - Moira can't help but make everything about her theatrical skills. The foreign language adds pretension and chaos to their simple song.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Ahhhhh, mon dieu! I simply must— *joins in with elaborate operatic flourishes in mock French and Italian*

tmdb_overview: Johnny puts the motel in jeopardy when he rents rooms to a dubious enterprise and David helps Moira with a film audition.
insight: David's interior design obsession drives 18 of 60 jokes, anchoring the episode's satirical backbone.
